description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by two prominent rust-colored metal hoops. The wood exhibits a natural, weathered grain with subtle variations in tone, giving it an authentic, aged appearance. The top and bottom are flat and appear to be made from the same wood as the staves, tightly fitted to prevent spillage. The metal bands are positioned near the top and bottom, providing structural reinforcement and a rustic aesthetic. There are no visible handles, spouts, or markings on the exterior, and the surface texture is rough and matte, typical of untreated or lightly finished wood. This type of barrel is commonly found in outdoor or semi-industrial urban settings, such as alleys, courtyards, or storage areas.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with two rust-colored metal hoops and a rough, matte finish. The barrel has a classic bulging shape and no visible handles or markings.
description_view_0: The barrel is seen from the front, showing the full curvature of the staves and both metal hoops clearly.
description_view_1: This view highlights the wood grain and the alignment of the staves, with the metal hoops still visible but less prominent.
description_view_2: The barrel appears slightly rotated, emphasizing the seamless join of the staves and the even placement of the hoops.
description_view_3: This angle shows the opposite side, with similar features and no additional markings or handles visible.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: metal hoops near top and bottom
